title: potentilla hirta subvar. ophiolithica
intro: Potentilla hirta subvar. ophiolithica, commonly known as a variety of cinquefoil, is a flowering plant native to mountainous regions, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Potentilla hirta subvar. ophiolithica include reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal properties, and its dried roots and leaves can be used in herbal remedies.
